summary: EXP-299 (two-patch defense neutralization) preempts the WIN-033 pole-region defender pivot. P1 appends a labeled "Defender pivot anticipated" paragraph to detail_verdict_text with three counters (the dome's own n(r) IS continuous and radial; Canopus at δ=-52.7° maps to r≈15,860 km / 79% to rim on the dome's own azimuthal-equidistant disc; the pole-only retreat proves too much by becoming unfalsifiable). P2 sets the previously-null detail_extra with three substantive paragraphs: precession argument (~25,772-yr cycle moves south pole toward Canopus by ~14,000 AD, dissolving the asymmetry the dome cites as confirmation), OPEN-004 cross-reference (the dome's own model docs admit the cutoff formula is underived, making CONFIRMED status against an underived expectation definitionally retrodictive), and color-test escape-hatch closure (any n(r) extinction must produce hemispheric color excess; Hipparcos and Gaia DR3 photometry of 1.8 billion stars see none). Curmudgeon C3 holes #3 (gradient framing missing) and #4 (precession argument missing) and stronger_arguments #1 (color test) and #3 (OPEN-004) all addressed. Holes #1 (Gaia DR3 vs EDR3 citation) and #2 (Polaris L=1260 vs d=433ly inconsistency) were already closed in current text and are not modified — verified by reading detail_verdict_text and finding before this run. ISS-1920 should close to fixed after patch application.
